title: Create and Configure Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling with Launch Templates

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.

tasks.title: Task 2: Create a Security Group for Launch template

steps.text: Make sure you are in the N.Virginia Region.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu available under the Compute section.
On the left panel menu, select the security group under the Network & Security section.
Click on the Create Security Group.

We are going to create a Security group for the Launch template with port 80 number enabled. Security group name: Enter Launch-template-SG Description: Enter Security group for Launch template VPC: Select Default VPC
We are going to create a Security group for the Launch template with port 80 number enabled.
Security group name: Enter Launch-template-SG
Description: Enter Security group for Launch template
VPC: Select Default VPC
Task 2: Create a Security Group for Launch template
Click on the Add rule under Inbound rules. Type : Select HTTP Source : Select Anywhere-IPv4 In the textbox add 0.0.0.0/0
Click on the Add rule under Inbound rules.
Type : Select HTTP
Source : Select Anywhere-IPv4
In the textbox add 0.0.0.0/0
Leave everything as default and click on the Create Security Group.
Task 2: Create a Security Group for Launch template
In the left navigation pane (scroll down) within Network & Security, click on the Key Pairs.
To create a new key pair, click on the Create Key Pair.
Fill in the details below: Name: Enter WhizKeyPair File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users) Leave other options as default. Click on the Create Key Pair.
Fill in the details below:
Name: Enter WhizKeyPair
File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users)
Leave other options as default.
Click on the Create Key Pair.
Task 2: Create a Security Group for Launch template
Key pair will be created.

tasks.title: Task 3: Create a Key pair for the Launch template

steps.text: In the left navigation pane (scroll down) within Network & Security, click on the Key Pairs.
To create a new key pair, click on the Create Key Pair.
Fill in the details below: Name: Enter WhizKeyPair File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users) Leave other options as default. Click on the Create Key Pair.
Fill in the details below:
Name: Enter WhizKeyPair
File format: pem (Linux & Mac Users) or ppk (Windows users)
Leave other options as default.
Click on the Create Key Pair.
Task 3: Create a Key pair for the Launch template
Key pair will be created.
In the left navigation pane (scroll down) within Instances, click on the Launch Templates
Click on the Create Launch Template
Under Launch template name and description section: Launch template name: Enter labLC Template version description: Type Launch template for whizdemo Leave other options as default.
Under Launch template name and description section:
Launch template name: Enter labLC
Template version description: Type Launch template for whizdemo
Leave other options as default.
Under Launch template contents: Amazon machine image (AMI): Select Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI. Under Instance type: Select t2.micro from the below list.
Under Launch template contents:
Amazon machine image (AMI): Select Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI.
Under Instance type: Select t2.micro from the below list.
Select t2.micro from the below list.
Task 4: Creating a Launch template
Key pair (Login): Select WhizKeyPair
Task 4: Creating a Launch template
Networking settings: Firewall(security groups): Select Select existing security group Security groups: Select Launch-template-SG from the drop-down
Networking settings:
Firewall(security groups): Select Select existing security group
Security groups: Select Launch-template-SG from the drop-down
Task 4: Creating a Launch template
Leave all other options as default.
Expand the option of Advance details, Go to the User data at the bottom of the page and paste the below script.
To create, click on the Create launch template. Upon successful creation, it will create a Launch template. Click on View launch templates button.
Task 4: Creating a Launch template

tasks.title: Task 5: Create an Auto Scaling Group

steps.text: An Auto Scaling group is a scalable collection of EC2 instances. When you create an Auto Scaling group, you include information such as the subnets for the instances and the number of instances the group must maintain at all times.
Go to the left menu under EC2 and choose Auto Scaling Groups under Auto Scaling.
Click on the Create Auto Scaling Group.
Step 1 : Choose launch template Auto Scaling group name : Enter Whiz-ASG Select the Launch template labLC from the list. Leave everything as default and click on the Next
Step 1 : Choose launch template
Auto Scaling group name : Enter Whiz-ASG
Select the Launch template labLC from the list. Leave everything as default and click on the Next
Step 2: Choose instance launch options
VPC: Select the Default VPC from the list.
Subnet: Select any two subnets for your Auto Scaling instances.
Click on the Next
Task 5: Create an Auto Scaling Group
Step 3: Integrate with other services
No changes needed in this page, click on the Next
Step 4: Configure group size and scaling
Under Group size - optional
Desired capacity : Enter 2
Under Scaling:
Minimum capacity : Enter 2
Maximum capacity : Enter 2
Task 5: Create an Auto Scaling Group
Under Automatic Scaling - optional Select No scaling policies
Under Automatic Scaling - optional
Select No scaling policies
Under Instance scale-in protection No changes are needed, click on the Next
Under Instance scale-in protection
No changes are needed, click on the Next
Step 5: Add notifications No changes are needed in this page, click on the Next
Step 5: Add notifications
No changes are needed in this page, click on the Next
Step 6: Add tags Enter tags in key-value pairs to identify your auto scaling group instances. Key : Enter Name Value : Enter Whiz Click on the Next
Step 6: Add tags
Enter tags in key-value pairs to identify your auto scaling group instances. Key : Enter Name Value : Enter Whiz
Enter tags in key-value pairs to identify your auto scaling group instances.
Key : Enter Name
Value : Enter Whiz
Click on the Next
Now Review, scroll down and click on the Create Auto Scaling Group.
You will be redirected to the autoscaling group page, you will be able to see that two instances are launched by the autoscaling groups.
Task 5: Create an Auto Scaling Group

Now go to the EC2 instances list. You will see that there are two new running instances (which were created by your autoscaling group) You can confirm this from their tag name, which you gave at the time of creating the autoscaling group.
Task 5: Create an Auto Scaling Group
You have successfully created an autoscaling group with a policy to a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 2 instances.
For testing the auto-scaling policy, go to the EC2 instance list and select one of your instances.
To terminate the EC2 instances manually: Select one of the instances randomly Click on the Instance state Choose to terminate the instance by clicking on Terminate instance.
To terminate the EC2 instances manually:
Select one of the instances randomly
Click on the Instance state
Choose to terminate the instance by clicking on Terminate instance.
Confirm the termination by clicking on the Terminate button.
This will terminate your instance.
Once your instance is terminated. You can go to the Auto scaling groups page and see the Instances is 1 and the Status is Updating capacity
Task 6: Test Auto Scaling Group
Alternatively, you can look for the Activity history under Activity of your Auto scaling group to see what happened.
Meanwhile, A new instance will be launched to fulfill the policy condition. A sample screenshot is provided below:
Task 6: Test Auto Scaling Group
Task 7: Validation Test
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 6: Test Auto Scaling Group
You have created a security group and key pair for the Launch template.
You created a Launch template and Auto Scaling.
You have launched two EC2 instances through Auto scaling.
You have tested the Auto scaling by terminating one of the instances manually.
Sign out of the AWS Account
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
